meaning: n.(门、盖等的)铰链,枢纽,关键vt.装铰链v.装以绞链,依...而转移
examples: Her ire slid between us like the spanner from Father's workshop, straining our hinge.
By varying the width of the black lines, or hinges, researchers are able to change how far each hinge folds.
The wider the hinge, the further it folds.
Shows hinge entirely on audience suggestions, and each turn can run 40 minutes or longer.
Its plans hinge on eliminating the largest cause of road accidents -- the drivers themselves.
